output: Batch 2 complete. Implemented the SQLite editorial migration, JSON column registration, Supabase truncate update, parser regression tests, no-epic loop/CLI path, and envelope schema regression test. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/sprint-2a-editorial-core/execution_batch_2.json`.

deviations: Used `python -m pytest` for verification because the bare `pytest` shim was established in batch 1 as Python 3.8 while this project requires Python >=3.11.

Full suite under Python 3.11 stopped at the same pre-existing `tests/test_no_leaked_secrets.py::test_leaked_supabase_service_role_jwt_prefix_is_absent` failure, reporting leaked Supabase JWT prefixes in `.megaplan/plans/sprint-1b-discord-resident/*`. This batch did not modify those files.
`python -m pytest tests/test_supabase_store.py -q` skipped because `SUPABASE_TEST_DB_URL` is not set; static checks verified the truncate block edit.
Advisory quality: agent_kit/store/sqlite.py has similar functions mark_failed and mark_orphaned (85% similarity).
Advisory quality: agent_kit/loop.py adds unused imports: agent_kit.tools.communication, agent_kit.tools.images.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added SQLite 004_editorial_core.sql with checklist_items and epic_events, CHECK constraints, FK behavior, indexes, and prior_state as TEXT. Added prior_state to _JSON_COLUMNS in both SQLiteStore and SupabaseStore. Verified by static checks and SQLite migration smoke against a fresh database.

Added checklist_items and epic_events to the Supabase truncate block before parent tables. Static check confirmed both names are in the TRUNCATE block before epics. Live Supabase test file skips without SUPABASE_TEST_DB_URL.

Added parser tests covering six roundtrip fixtures, section ops preserving untouched sections, _preamble title replacement/removal validation, five lenient parse cases, fenced ## as preamble-only, and diffs_equivalent boundary cases. The full test file passes.

Updated run_turn to accept epic_id=None, skip lock/hot-context when no epic exists, carry active_epic_id through envelope/abort paths, capture inbound_message_id, and refresh active_epic_id after every registry.invoke call. Existing CLI/run_turn modules and no-epic CLI smoke pass.
commands_run: python -m py_compile agent_kit/loop.py arnold/cli.py agent_kit/store/sqlite.py agent_kit/store/supabase.py

task_updates.executor_notes: Made --epic optional with default None and help text documenting no-epic bootstrap. Existing run path and exception envelope continue to pass args.epic through; no-epic CLI smoke returned a valid completed envelope without epic_id.

Added envelope schema regression test for Envelope(epic_id=None), validating both omitted epic_id from to_json/_drop_none and explicit epic_id:null against envelope.schema.json. The full envelope test file passes.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. SQLite migration mirrors the editorial tables using SQLite-compatible types with prior_state TEXT, and prior_state is registered in _JSON_COLUMNS in both adapters.
Yes. checklist_items and epic_events are in the TRUNCATE block before parent tables they reference; the Supabase test module skips without SUPABASE_TEST_DB_URL.
Yes. tests/test_body_parser.py covers all requested roundtrip, _preamble, lenient parse, fence, section-op, and diff equivalence cases.

Yes. active_epic_id is local state for envelope/abort paths, lock acquisition is skipped when epic_id is None, no-epic hot context is synthesized, inbound_message_id is captured, and active_epic_id refreshes after both registry.invoke calls.
Yes. --epic now defaults to None, help documents omitted --epic bootstrap, and the CLI smoke confirms args.epic=None produces a valid no-epic envelope.
Yes. The regression test constructs Envelope(epic_id=None), serializes through to_json(), validates omitted epic_id, then validates an explicit epic_id:null payload.
